metadata.title: Rowlabeler - Excel Sequence Generator for Bulk Row Labels
metadata.description: Generate Excel row labels, account IDs, SKU sequences, formulas, text previews, and CSV output without dragging hundreds of thousands of rows.
metadata.keywords: excel sequence generator, row label generator, excel formula generator, account label generator, csv sequence generator

hero.title: Generate Excel sequences without dragging 400,000 rows
hero.description: Create bulk labels like Account1, Account2, row numbers, zero-padded IDs, Excel formulas, and CSV previews from one focused generator.

announcement.title: Built for spreadsheet cleanup, ops lists, and bulk numbering

generator.title: Generate labels, formulas, and CSV previews in one place
generator.label: Generator
generator.description: Enter the sequence pattern once. Copy an Excel 365 formula, a legacy ROW formula, a plain-text preview, or a CSV preview without dragging the fill handle.

labels.previewNote: Showing the first {limit} rows only to keep the browser fast.
labels.emptyState: Fix the inputs to generate formulas and previews.

errorMessages.prefix-required: Enter a prefix such as Account, Item, SKU, or Row.
errorMessages.start-invalid: Start number must be an integer.
errorMessages.count-invalid: Row count must be between 1 and 1,000,000.
errorMessages.step-invalid: Step must be a non-zero integer.
errorMessages.separator-invalid: Separator must be 10 characters or fewer and cannot contain line breaks.
errorMessages.output-columns-invalid: CSV columns must be between 1 and 26.

use-cases.title: Built around the boring Excel jobs that waste real time
use-cases.label: Use Cases
use-cases.description: Most users do not need another spreadsheet course. They need the right formula, a safe preview, and something they can paste back into Excel.

items.title: Account1 to Account400000
items.description: Generate long account, ticket, invoice, batch, or SKU labels without dragging the fill handle down the sheet.

items.title: Excel formula generator
items.description: Get formula patterns for modern Excel 365 and older Excel versions where SEQUENCE is unavailable.

items.title: CSV and plain-text preview
items.description: Preview a limited slice of the output before copying formulas or exporting values into another workflow.

workflow.title: From numbering idea to paste-ready output
workflow.description: A simple three-step path for people who only opened Excel because something had to be numbered.

items.title: Describe the sequence
items.description: Choose the label prefix, starting number, row count, increment, separator, and padding.

items.title: Review formulas and preview
items.description: Compare the Excel 365 formula, legacy formula, and a short generated preview before copying.

items.title: Paste into Excel
items.description: Use the formula for dynamic sheets or copy values when you need fixed labels for import/export.

features.title: Focused on bulk numbering
features.description: Open the page, generate formulas, preview the output, and paste the result back into Excel without accounts, uploads, or extra setup.

items.title: Deterministic output
items.description: No AI guessing. The same inputs produce the same formulas and previews every time.

items.title: Excel-aware formulas
items.description: Support for SEQUENCE formulas and legacy ROW-based alternatives.

items.title: Copy-first UI
items.description: Copy buttons, preview limits, and error messages are built around real spreadsheet work.

items.title: Fits common label jobs
items.description: Use it for accounts, tickets, SKUs, batches, row labels, and other repetitive sequences.

stats.title: Less dragging, more certainty
stats.description: Describe the numbering rule once, then let Excel formulas and previews handle the repetitive work.

items.description: Formula-ready for very large row counts

faq.title: Excel sequence generator FAQ
faq.description: Short answers for users searching from Excel forums, Reddit, and search engines.

items.question: Can this generate Account1, Account2, Account3 style labels?
items.answer: Yes. Enter a prefix, start number, count, step, separator, and padding, then copy the formula, preview values, or CSV output.

items.question: Will it work without Excel 365?
items.answer: Yes. The page gives you both a modern SEQUENCE formula and a legacy ROW-based formula, so you can choose the pattern that fits your Excel version.

items.question: Can I generate hundreds of thousands of rows?
items.answer: The tool will generate formulas for large row counts while keeping browser previews limited, so the page stays fast and usable.

items.question: Does the site upload or edit my spreadsheet file?
items.answer: No. It generates text and formulas in the browser. It does not require spreadsheet uploads, accounts, payments, or direct workbook editing.

cta.title: Generate your Excel sequence now
cta.description: Enter a prefix and row count, copy the formula that fits your Excel version, or download CSV output for the next step.
